results.title: Former Bungoma governor Wangamati joins Linda Mwananchi
results.description: Former Bungoma Governor Wafula Wangamati has officially joined the Linda Mwananchi movement led by Nairobi Senator Edwin Sifuna. Speaking during preparations for Edwin Sifuna’s upcoming homecoming ceremony in Bungoma County on Friday, July 17, 2026, the former governor announced that he had fully joined Sifuna’s movement and would lead campaigns across Bungoma County and the [...]

results.title: When The Church Offering Plate Becomes A Weapon: Seven Signs Of Spiritual Manipulation
results.description: A Mississippi pastor recently ignited a firestorm after reportedly posting the names of church members on Facebook who had failed to pay their tithes. Whether the intention was accountability, encouragement, or frustration, the reaction was swift. Christians from across the theological spectrum condemned the move as public shaming rather than biblical shepherding. The controversy raises a much larger question than one pastor's judgment. It forces Christians to ask an uncomfortable but necessary question: Where is the line between teaching biblical stewardship and manipulating God's people?The Bible has much to say about generosity. Jesus spoke often about money because it reveals...

results.title: Hyderabad sanitation suffers as workers join voter revision
results.description:
HYDERABAD: Sanitation work has been affected in several areas under the three municipal corporations the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC), MPN and CMC — for the past 10 days. Garbage has accumulated in many colonies and slum areas as sanitation workers have been deployed for the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. Officials are using [...]HYDERABAD: Sanitation work has been affected in several areas under the three municipal corporations the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC), MPN and CMC — for the past 10 days. Garbage has accumulated in many colonies and slum areas as sanitation workers have been deployed for the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls.

Officials are using sanitation workers to complete the SIR exercise. In several circles, they have been assigned the distribution of enumeration forms. Educated sanitation workers and Sanitary Field Assistants (SFAs) are also serving as Booth Level Officers (BLOs) in some locations. This has affected routine sanitation services.

Staff shortage worsens waste management

The three municipal corporations already face a shortage of sanitation workers. Even the sanctioned workforce is not fully available on the ground. Despite the use of biometric and facial recognition attendance systems, many workers do not report for duty regularly. Some remain absent, while others mark attendance and leave.

Because of the staff shortage, daily sweeping is largely confined to main roads, while internal roads in colonies and slums are often left unattended. The additional deployment of sanitation workers for SIR duties has further worsened the situation.

Residents complain over unclean roads

Residents have complained to senior officials about roads not being swept for nearly a week. However, they say no action has been taken. Overall, the ongoing Special Intensive Revision exercise has had a significant impact on sanitation services across the three municipal corporations. ]]>

results.title: DOJ says it's no longer illegal to download TikTok on federal devices
results.description: The Justice Department determined this week a federal law banning TikTok from government devices no longer applies to the social video app.

results.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Aminuddin faces three-cornered battle in Linggi
results.description: PORT DICKSON: The Linggi seat is set for a three-cornered contest in the Negri Sembilan election, with the incumbent, caretaker menteri besar and a former contender for the seat set to face off in a closely watched battle.
